description: Calculate the Location Quotient (LQ) for retail and hospitality sectors in San Francisco to identify organic commercial agglomerations using the Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/retail_agglomeration.ipynb. Process the jobs, buildings, and parcels data from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5 to determine the Retail LQ for each zone, defining retail jobs as those with sector_id 10 or 11. The LQ is calculated as (Zone Retail Jobs / Zone Total Jobs) / (Citywide Retail Jobs / Citywide Total Jobs), with 0 assigned for division-by-zero cases and NaN values filled with 0. Identify 'Retail Centers' as zones meeting the criteria of LQ > 1.25 and retail_jobs >= 100. Export the complete zone-level analysis (columns: zone_id, total_jobs, retail_jobs, lq, is_retail_center) to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zone_lq_analysis.csv and a filtered list of Retail Centers (sorted by LQ descending) to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/retail_centers.csv. Create and save a scatter plot of Total Jobs vs Retail LQ, highlighting the Retail Centers, to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/retail_lq_plot.png. The final notebook must be fully executed.
